data.title: First Tick: Here are the top global cues for today’s trade
data.description:
Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty 50 are likely to open higher on February 6, tracking cues from GIFT Nifty trading around 23,801.5, a short while ago this morning.

Track the latest updates on GIFT Nifty right here on Moneycontrol.

Indian markets closed lower on February 5, dragged down by losses in consumer stocks amid weak earnings. Investor sentiment remained cautious ahead of the Reserve Bank of India's key bi-monthly policy announcement scheduled for February 7.

The benchmark Sensex declined 0.4 percent, shedding 312 points to close at 78,271, while the Nifty settled 0.18 percent lower, losing 43 points to end at 23,696.30.

Here is how financial markets across the globe fared overnight:

GIFT Nifty (Up)

The GIFT Nifty is a trading firm, indicating a positive start for the day. Nifty futures were trading at 23,801.5 at around 07:00 am IST.

Asian Equities (Gain)Asian equities advanced Thursday after stocks and bonds rose on Wall Street in a week marred by

tariffs, lackluster tech earnings and uneven US economic data. Shares in Australia, Japan and South Korea rose, while equity futures for Hong Kong also advanced.

US Equities (Up)

US stocks ended a see-saw session with gains and benchmark Treasury yields slid on Wednesday as disappointing earnings and mixed economic data counterbalanced easing jitters of a spreading global trade war.

All three major U.S. stock indexes closed in positive territory, but the tech-heavy Nasdaq's nominal gain was held in check by disappointing earnings from Alphabet, which fueled doubts about the payoff of investment in artificial intelligence.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 317.24 points, or 0.71%, to 44,873.28, the S&P 500 rose 23.60 points, or 0.39%, to 6,061.48 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 38.32 points, or 0.19%, to 19,692.33.

US Bond Yield (Rises)

The yield on 10-year Treasuries was little changed at 4.42%, while US 2-Year Treasury was up 10 bps at 4.19 percent in the early trade on Thursday.

Dollar Index (Flat)Lower yields weighed on the dollar. An index of greenback strength fell 0.2% to the lowest level in a week, undoing the gains spurred on by news of Donald Trump’s tariffs on Mexico,

Canada and China over the past week that initially boosted demand for defensive assets

Asian currencies (Mixed)

Asian currencies were trading mixed in the early Thursday trade with Malaysian Ringgit, China Renminbi, South Korean Won leading the losers, while Indonesian Rupiah, Japanese Yen and Philippines Peso among the major gainers.

Crude (Gains)

In commodities, oil prices trade marginally higher on Thursday after falling more than 2 percent in the previous session.

Gold (Up)Gold steadied in early Asian trading — after blowing through a series of record highs in the previous session — with rising economic and geopolitical risks supporting haven

demand.

Fund Flow Action

The Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) turned net sellers on February 5, as they sold equities worth Rs 1,682.83 crore, while domestic institutional investors (DIIs) purchased equities worth Rs 996.28 crore.

Hope you're all set for today's trade, we wish you a profitable day ahead.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Lightning Minerals to Start Diamond Drilling at Brazil Project; Shares Up 9%
data.description:
Lightning Minerals is set to start a diamond drill program for up to 2,000 meters at the Esperança lithium project in Brazil in mid-February, with mobilization underway, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company engaged Energold Drilling as the preferred contractor for the drilling program, targeting spodumene within pegmatites and lithium-in-soil anomalies discovered in November 2024, the filing said.

Meanwhile, ground reconnaissance is ongoing at both the Caraíbas and Canabrava projects, also located in Brazil, per the filing.

Shares rose nearly 9% in midday trade Thursday.

data.title: Gold Hit Record High Amid Trade War Concerns, ANZ Research Says
data.description:
Trade war concerns boosted gold to a high of $2,882 per ounce earlier as investors scrambled for physical deliveries, according to a Thursday report from ANZ Research.

Gold rose 0.9% to $2,868.70 per ounce as of Thursday.

Traders are concerned that the US could place tariffs on precious metal imports into the US following President Donald Trump's recent announcements of tariffs against Canada, Mexico, and China. Trump then delayed the tariffs imposed on Canada and Mexico.

The report noted that there was gold withdrawal from the Bank of England's vaults, a key lender to the industry, faced waiting times of a week.

Gold in the Bank of England's vault is trading at a discount of more than $5 per ounce below spot prices to the wider market.

One-month lease rates have jumped to about 4.7%.

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Rise, Tracking Wall Street Gains — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rises 0.3% to 20661.51, tracking Wall Street gains. Markets are likely stabilizing as tariff-related news took a breather, the UOB Global Economics & Markets Research team writes in a note. Lenovo Group rises 4.0%, Techtronic Industries gains 3.5% and Nongfu Spring adds 3.0%. Meanwhile, Baidu falls 1.4%, AIA Group loses 0.85% and WH Group is 0.6% lower. The Hang Seng Tech Index is 0.4% higher at 4950.88. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Mkventures Capital Appoints CFO
data.description:
Mkventures Capital has appointed Shyam Jaju as chief financial officer and key managerial personnel of the company, with effect Wednesday, according to a same-day disclosure to the BSE.

Jaju takes over from Rashmee Purushottam Mehta, who tendered his resignation, effective Wednesday, citing personal reasons and his health condition.

data.title: Carl's Jr. Returns to Racy Advertising Years After It Abandoned 'Bikinis and Burgers' — WSJ
data.description:
By Patrick Coffee

Carl's Jr. has updated its old sexualized marketing strategy with an ad starring influencer Alix Earle, years after the burger chain stopped making suggestive ads and promised to focus more on its food.

Earle wears little clothing as she promotes a bulging new "hangover burger" in the Super Bowl-themed ad, reprising the role once played in Carl's Jr. campaigns by such names as Paris Hilton and Kim Kardashian — but now with some self-referential humor from a TikTok star tossed in.

The new twist on the brand's once-signature style mirrors a cultural about-face that coincides with President Trump's return to office, according to Tim Calkins, clinical professor of marketing and associate chair of the marketing department at Northwestern's Kellogg School of Management.

"I think it does reflect a shift in how the country is feeling," said Calkins. "Clearly with the new administration ... what is acceptable conduct is changing, and I think you're seeing Carl's Jr. jump right on that trend as one of the first brands that's really pushing the edge on things like this."

This ad almost certainly wouldn't have run several years ago, when American culture focused more intently on matters of gender equality and diversity, Calkins said.

The new campaign is intended to evoke nostalgia for its predecessors while adding humor to target a core audience of Gen Z consumers who live — and order hamburgers — online, said Jennifer Tate, chief marketing officer at Carl's Jr. parent company CKE Restaurants, which also owns the restaurant chain Hardee's.

A carwash featured early in the video, for example, is a subtle nod to a 2005 Paris Hilton commercial in the original wave of ads.

Earle's appeal lies in her ability to feature both the highlights and foibles of her life online, Tate said, referring to the influencer's "unapologetic, bold style."

"This is the new world with new rules and new rulers. Alix Earle is a social media empress," Tate said in a statement. "Carl's Jr. is having so much fun doing things other brands are too timid to do."

'Bikinis and burgers'

Carl's Jr. began its shift away from its racy ads in 2017, saying the strategy was distracting from the chain's food.

"Since premiering our now iconic ad starring Paris Hilton 12 years ago, Carl's Jr. and Hardee's have become synonymous with two things: bikinis and burgers," said Brad Haley, then the CMO at CKE Restaurants, in a press release that year. That made it difficult to tell a broader story about menu quality, Haley said.

Even before that, Carl's Jr. had defended its steamy ads in a defiant 2011 press release that has since been removed from CKE Restaurants' web site.

"We believe in putting hot models in our commercials, because ugly ones don't sell burgers," the release read, according to a copy archived online. "That's just the way it is."

For the second year in a row, Carl's Jr. is promoting its loyalty program by offering free burgers to members the day after the Super Bowl. The company will gauge this campaign's success by monitoring sign-ups and online sales, as well as bricks-and-mortar foot traffic, Tate said.

Like past campaigns tied to the Super Bowl, this one also aims to attract an outsize share of attention — but without costing the company the millions of dollars in media fees that an actual national Super Bowl commercial would cost. The ad will run on traditional and streaming TV only in some markets, and not in-game, said Tate.

The campaign will also lean heavily on social media, Tate said. Earle shared the ad with her 7.2 million TikTok followers on Wednesday.

The brand will release more material with Earle later this year, Tate said.

Write to Patrick Coffee at patrick.coffee@wsj.com

data.title: Pure Hydrogen Delivers Hydrogen Fuel Cell-Powered Truck to Waste Management Firm
data.description:
Pure Hydrogen completed the handover of a hydrogen fuel cell-powered rear loader garbage truck to client waste management firm Solo Resource Recover, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The hydrogen fuel cell-powered rear loader garbage truck is a first in Australia, the filing said.

The truck will begin operations initially in Adelaide in South Australia, following a launch in Victoria, per the filing.

The firm confirmed that it received Australia Design Rule approval for this type of truck, allowing it to automatically register new vehicles of this type.

data.title: BOJ's Tamura makes his case again for faster interest rate hikes
data.description:
Bank of Japan

  • Worried that sharp rises in rice prices, coupled with inflation exceeding 2% lasting for nearly three years, could hurt consumption.
  • Output gap may already effectively be in positive territory as supply constraints put upward pressure on prices.
  • Corporate and household inflation expectations are heightening, having roughly reached levels around 2%.
  • Upward price risks are building up.
  • The BOJ must raise rates to levels deemed neutral on a nominal basis, which is at least around 1%.
  • The BOJ must raise rates at least to around 1% in the latter half of fiscal 2025.
  • The BOJ must raise rates in several stages in a timely fashion while scrutinising what the appropriate short-term rate level could be for the economy.
  • Even if BOJ hikes rates to 0.75%, real interest rates will remain deeply negative.

more to come

data.title: Betmakers Technology Unit Makes AU$1.5 Million Tech Acquisition; Shares up 5%
data.description:
Betmakers Technology unit BetMakers MTC has acquired intellectual property and software from Protocol Zone for a total consideration of AU$1.5 million, the company said in a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

Out of the total of AU$1.5 million, Betmakers will pay AU$100,000 in cash upon certain BetMakers products going live but no later than April 7 and another AU$100,000 in company shares. The remaining $1.3 million will be paid in monthly installments from a percentage of increased net revenues generated by the new GTX software and related products using the intellectual property, per the filing.

Protocol Zone Managing Director Kartik Modi has agreed to a three-year exclusive contract with BetMakers as their Country Manager in India, where he will oversee operations and explore opportunities in the Asia region, the filing added.

Company shares were up nearly 5% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: NextEd Group CFO, Chair to Retire; Shares Rise 4%
data.description:
NextEd Group Chief Financial Officer Michael Fahey and Chair Cass O'Connor are set retire, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

O'Connor will depart on March 3 and will be succeeded by Sandra Hook, who chairs the nominations and remuneration committee, the filing said.

Fahey will remain with the company until the end of April. A search for his successor is underway, per the filing.

The private education provider's earnings report is slated for release on Feb. 26.

NextEd's shares were up 4% in recent trade.

Oil Edges Higher; Rising Stockpiles, Trade Uncertainty to Weigh — Market Talk
data.description: Oil edges higher in early Asian trade, after falling overnight as data showed a weekly climb of almost 9 million barrels in U.S. crude inventories. Crude oil prices may continue to be weighed by rising U.S. stockpiles and trade-related uncertainty, says Tickmill's Joseph Dahrieh in a note. This reinforces a bearish sentiment due to persistent demand-side risks, the managing principal writes. Although supply constraints would provide some stability, expectations of rising production in the U.S. and from OPEC members could weigh on the market over the longer-term, he says. Front-month WTI crude oil futures rise 0.2% to $71.20/bbl; front-month Brent crude oil futures are up 0.1% to $74.71/bbl.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Indonesia's Steady Growth Momentum May Keep Central Bank in Wait-and-See Mode — Market Talk
data.description: Indonesia's 1Q GDP growth is expected to remain steady, supported by increased government social spending that could boost household consumption, CIMB economists say in a note. The economists keep Indonesia's 2025 GDP growth estimate at 5.0%. With supportive growth and external uncertainties, Bank Indonesia is likely to take a wait-and-see approach, balancing stability and growth while strategically reducing the policy rate only when a favorable window arises, they say. CIMB maintains its forecast for one 25bps rate cut per quarter, bringing the policy rate to 5.00% by end-2025.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Shenzhen Composite Index Opens 0.2% Lower at 1915.61
Shanghai Composite Index Opens 0.1% Lower at 3226.58
Most JGBs Edge Higher, Tracking Price Gains in U.S. Treasurys — Market Talk
data.description: Most JGBs edge higher in the morning Tokyo session, tracking price gains in U.S. Treasurys overnight. Both JGBs and Treasurys tend to move in tandem. Today's auction of 900 billion yen in 30-year sovereign debt by Japan's Finance Ministry is likely to be digested smoothly and the 30-year yield is expected to hover around 2.3%, Citi Research's Tomohisa Fujiki says in a research report. Given the Bank of Japan's schedule of JGB purchase operations, investors may also prefer yield-curve flatteners, the rates strategist adds. The JGB 30-year yield is down 1 bps at 2.315%; 10-year yield is down 0.5 bps at 1.275%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: ChiNext Price Index Opens 0.2% Lower at 2059.45
REA Reports 'Very Strong' Fiscal First-Half Results, Details on Costs Guidance Downgrade Required, Jarden Research Says
data.description:
REA Group reported "very strong" fiscal first-half results, and downgraded its fiscal year cost guidance, now expecting low double-digit core operating cost growth, from the previous high single-digit forecast, according to a Thursday note by Jarden Research,

The digital advertising company reported Thursday that group revenue for the six months to Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$872.9 million, up 20% from AU$725.5 million a year earlier, beating Jarden's AU$869 million guidance.

Group earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization before associates was AU$535 million, up 22% from last year, and 1.7% above the market's expectations of AU$527 million, Jarden noted.

Jarden estimates a AU$12 million to AU$20 million increase to REA's fiscal 2025 cost outlook, with first-half costs already AU$10m above consensus, implying an AU$2 million to AU$10m rise in costs for the fiscal second half.

Further clarification is needed on whether the cost downgrade is due to higher revenue expectations or a changed revenue mix, Jarden said.

Jarden Research has an underweight rating on REA Group with an AU$198 price target.

data.title: South Korea's Inflation Picks Up in January
data.description:
South Korea's inflation rate rose 2.2% in January from a year earlier, marking the fastest on-year growth in five months, according to Statistics Korea on Thursday.

January's figure was the highest inflation rate since July last year when the index reached 2.6%.

The index increased 0.7% from December 2024.

The increase was due to an uptick in the prices of food and non-alcoholic beverages, housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels, furnishings, household equipment and routine maintenance, health, transport, recreation and culture, education, restaurants and hotels, and miscellaneous goods and services.

The core consumer price index, which excludes food and energy prices, rose 0.5% in January from a month prior and increased 1.9% from a year ago.

data.title: Native Mineral Resources Plans Queensland Gold Project Drilling Program; Shares Reach Near Two-Year High
data.description:
Native Mineral Resources Holdings is planning an 18-hole diamond drilling program at the Blackjack gold project in Queensland, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

It plans to restart production at the project in the third quarter of 2025.

The firm brought in MEC Mining to review historical drilling at the project. The review indicated the presence of anomalous mineralization within 50 meters of the surface of the project.

Native Mineral's shares rose almost 8% in recent trading on Thursday and earlier hit their highest since March 2023.
